ac_2_6_compliance.tagging_contract: Each finding includes introduced_in_diff:true|false. Line-level findings include file:line plus diff-hunk overlap check. Per AC-2.6(b): a finding is provably NEW only when (i) its line falls within the cycle diff's changed-line range AND (ii) the finding is absent from the pre-diff baseline.
ac_2_6_compliance.pre_diff_baseline_source: git show HEAD:hooks/pretool-read-size-guard.py and git show HEAD:hooks/pretool-block-enterworktree.sh

advisory_findings.finding: Standard 6 unconditional scan (per agents/style-inspector.md) detected Chinese characters in commands/dev.md and commands/close.md. Verified via `git show HEAD:commands/dev.md` and `git show HEAD:commands/close.md` that ALL flagged occurrences predate this cycle. Many are verbatim user-binding quotes (e.g., <USER_VERBATIM>"又", "还是", "没修好", "第 N 次"</USER_VERBATIM>) or spec-citation quoted text — which fall under the documented Standard 6 exemption. Files are NOT in this cycle's --changed-files scope.
advisory_findings.recommendation: Out of scope for this cycle. No action required. Documented here only because the Standard 6 scan is unconditional per agent spec.

standard_1_no_inline_code.rationale: Both files are scripts (.py and .sh), not command/agent .md files. Standard 1 targets only command/agent markdown.

standard_2_no_hardcoded_values.notes: New code references /tmp/claude-orchestrator-consent-${SID}.flag and /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-${SID}.json. These are NOT hardcode violations — they are a cross-hook IPC contract that MUST match the exact paths used in pretool-orchestrator-gate.py:66-71, pretool-write-guard.sh:27/33, and other consent-aware hooks. Parameterization would break the contract. LINE_LIMIT/CHUNK_LIMIT are sensible module-level constants exposed at file top with clear names; the LINE_LIMIT 1000→600 change is the cosmetic L1 alignment described in the task brief.

standard_3_use_source_venv.notes: pretool-block-enterworktree.sh invokes `python3` directly (lines 17-19, 27-29, 31-34, 44). Hook scripts run in the harness PreToolUse context without a guaranteed venv. This pattern matches the reference idiom in pretool-write-guard.sh:18, 22, 35 (cited in the task brief) and is the established convention across all .claude/hooks/*.sh files. Architectural constraint, not a style violation.

standard_4_integer_step_numbering.rationale: No procedural step numbering in either file.

standard_5_meaningful_naming.notes: New symbol `has_consent` is descriptive. New `_BEW_*` variable prefix in the bash script matches the established `_WG_*` convention in pretool-write-guard.sh. No generic enhancement words (enhance-, fast-, optimize-, -v2, improved-, better-, new-).

standard_6_english_only.notes: Both in-scope files contain only English. Unconditional scan against agents/ and commands/ surfaced pre-existing Chinese in commands/dev.md and commands/close.md — confirmed pre-existing via git show HEAD: (introduced_in_diff:false, recorded in advisory_findings, out of cycle scope).

standard_7_merge_similar_scripts.rationale: Both files are pre-existing hooks being modified, not new scripts being added.

standard_8_concise_documentation.notes: New docstring in pretool-block-enterworktree.sh:7-11 is 5 lines, functional, and cross-references CLAUDE.md permanent-block list. No marketing language, no repetitive examples, no pseudocode.

standard_9_parameterized_scripts.notes: Hook scripts receive runtime input via stdin JSON (tool_name, session_id, tool_input, agent_id) — that IS the parameter mechanism. No fixed-value runtime parameters that should be configurable were introduced.

standard_10_dangling_references.notes: New `from lib.allowlist import read_grant` in both files resolves to /root/.claude/hooks/lib/allowlist.py (exists; `read_grant(tool_name, sid) -> bool` defined at line 130). Verified.

standard_11_merge_conflicts.notes: Grep for `<<<<<<<|=======|>>>>>>>` returned no matches in either file.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Skill tool blocked by /root/.claude/hooks/pretool-tool-policy.py for style-inspector role: 'tool Skill not in allowed_tools'. Codex delegation is not permitted for this role under the current tool-policy.v1 configuration. This is functionally equivalent to a 'codex unavailable' fallback case.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted — performed 5+ adversarial checks: (1) false positives? None — /tmp/ paths are documented cross-hook IPC contract, not hardcodes; LINE_LIMIT change is the declared L1 cosmetic alignment. (2) missed violations? None — re-walked every changed line against all 11 standards; the bash script's `_BEW_HOOKS_DIR=$(cd $(dirname ${BASH_SOURCE[0]}) && pwd)` is explicitly exempt per Standard 2 (script self-reference); python3 calls in .sh hooks match the cited reference idiom. (3) severity calibration? No violations to classify. (4) changed-files scope? Standard 6 unconditional scan results from outside the two in-scope files are tagged introduced_in_diff:false and recorded as advisory only; confirmed pre-existing via git show HEAD:. (5) wording accuracy? Re-read both files in full and the full diff hunks against the audit conclusions.
